pairs.instruction: Why does my torch go out faster when it's raining in Cubyz?

pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a boots-based fall damage reduction mechanic.

pairs.response: Adamantium isn't a real material in Cubyz.

pairs.response: Mythril isn't a real material in Cubyz.

pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have enchantment tables or an enchanting system.

pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have NPC shops or an in-game currency system.

pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a guild/clan system.

pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a PvP arena/queue system.

pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a documented spawn-protection-radius setting.

pairs.instruction: Does Cubyz support DLSS upscaling on Nvidia cards?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have documented DLSS support.

pairs.instruction: Why does Cubyz require a Steam account to launch?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't require a Steam account -- it's not distributed as a Steam-locked game.

pairs.instruction: How much does the mobile version of Cubyz cost?
pairs.response: There is no mobile version of Cubyz.

pairs.instruction: What's the minimum Vulkan version required to run Cubyz?
pairs.response: Cubyz's rendering requirements are OpenGL-based (4.3+), not a Vulkan version requirement.

pairs.instruction: Why does Cubyz need an internet connection to play singleplayer?
pairs.response: Cubyz singleplayer doesn't require an internet connection.

pairs.instruction: What parameters does the onBlockPlace() addon callback take?
pairs.response: There's no documented onBlockPlace() addon callback in Cubyz's addon API.

pairs.instruction: How do I register a custom particle effect in my addon's manifest.json?
pairs.response: Cubyz addons don't use a manifest.json file -- addon data is defined per-category in .zig.zon files.

pairs.instruction: What's the addon_version field format in the addon config?
pairs.response: There's no documented addon_version field in Cubyz's addon format.

pairs.instruction: How do I hook into the onWorldSave() event from an addon?
pairs.response: There's no documented onWorldSave() addon hook in Cubyz.

pairs.instruction: What's the /gamemode creative command syntax in Cubyz?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't use Minecraft-style /gamemode commands.

pairs.instruction: How do I set difficulty=hard in the world config?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a documented difficulty setting of that kind.

pairs.instruction: What's the tickSpeed config option's default value?
pairs.response: There's no documented tickSpeed config option in Cubyz.

pairs.instruction: Why did the original Cubyz team disband in 2020?
pairs.response: The Cubyz team hasn't disbanded -- it's an actively maintained project.

pairs.instruction: When did Cubyz switch from being closed-source to open-source?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a closed-source-to-open-source transition in its history that's documented.

pairs.response: Cubyz isn't developed by a paid team -- it's an open-source community project.

pairs.instruction: What major feature did Cubyz 1.0 add?
pairs.response: Cubyz hasn't reached a 1.0 release.

pairs.instruction: Why was multiplayer removed in the latest Cubyz update?
pairs.response: Multiplayer hasn't been removed from Cubyz -- it's an active, supported feature.

pairs.instruction: How do I open the skill tree menu?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a skill tree system or menu.

pairs.instruction: What key opens the world map overview?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a documented world-map-overview key/menu.

pairs.instruction: How do I customize my character's face in the appearance menu?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a character face-customization menu.

pairs.instruction: My game crashes because Cubyz doesn't support more than 8GB of RAM, how do I fix that?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have an 8GB RAM ceiling like that -- that's not a real limitation, so it's not the actual cause of the crash.

pairs.instruction: Since Cubyz caps FPS at 30 by default, how do I unlock higher framerates?
pairs.response: Cubyz doesn't have a default 30 FPS cap.

pairs.instruction: Cubyz's chunk loading is single-threaded, right? How do I enable multithreaded loading?
pairs.response: That's not accurate -- nothing in Cubyz's documented architecture describes chunk loading as single-threaded by default with a separate opt-in multithreaded mode.

pairs.instruction: Is it true there's currently no way to heal in Cubyz?
pairs.response: Yes -- there is currently no way to regain health in Cubyz other than dying.

pairs.instruction: Does Cubyz's world really extend 2 billion blocks in each direction?
pairs.response: Yes -- the Cubyz world can extend up to 2 billion blocks on each axis.

pairs.instruction: If I reference a block ID that doesn't exist in my addon, does the game crash?
pairs.response: No -- the engine doesn't crash. It logs "Couldn't find block {id}. Replacing it with air..." and substitutes air.

pairs.instruction: Is Cubyz really unsupported on Mac?
pairs.response: Yes -- Mac isn't supported because it lacks OpenGL 4.3.

pairs.instruction: Was Cubyz really called something else before its current name?
pairs.response: Yes -- Cubyz was originally called "Cubz."

pairs.instruction: Is the default multiplayer port for Cubyz really 47649?
pairs.response: Yes -- that's the default multiplayer server port.

pairs.instruction: Does the player really start with only 8 hearts in Cubyz?
pairs.response: Yes -- 8 hearts, 16 total health.

pairs.instruction: Would a Cubyz reviewer really prefer getOrPut over a separate contains-check plus insert?
pairs.response: Yes -- getOrPut atomically retrieves-or-inserts in one operation instead of two lookups, reducing redundant memory accesses and complexity.

pairs.instruction: Is it true a biome's custom sky color might not show up even if I set it correctly in the Addon Creator?
pairs.response: Yes -- that's a real format mismatch: the website exports skyColor/fogColor as a 3-component float vector, but the engine reads those fields as a packed u32 integer.

pairs.instruction: Did Cubyz really get rewritten from one programming language to another?
pairs.response: Yes -- Cubyz was rewritten from Java to Zig, largely due to Java's garbage collector causing lag spikes when the upper memory limit was hit.

pairs.instruction: Is there really a NeverFailingAllocator type in Cubyz's codebase?
pairs.response: Yes -- utils.NeverFailingAllocator is an allocator interface that cannot return error.OutOfMemory.

pairs.instruction: Is it true tools are used to damage soil blocks specifically, not just any tool?
pairs.response: Yes -- shovels are specifically the tool used to damage/mine soil blocks.
